channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania Expands Kiswahili Language Teaching with 20 Centres Abroad and 35 at Home
item.description: The Tanzanian government dey seriously promote Kiswahili language both within the country and internationally. Minister Paul Makonda announced say dem wan create opportunities for people in areas like...

item.title: Dollarama reports solid fiscal results but forecasts moderated same-store sales growth for the year ahead
item.description: Dollarama Inc. has released its financial results for the fiscal year ended February 1, showing continued growth despite some softening in consumer behaviour. The Montreal-based discount retailer repo...

item.title: Authorities seize P6.8M worth of shabu in separate Mindanao operations
item.description: COTABATO CITY, Philippines — Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA) agents, together with local police, successfully confiscated shabu worth a total of P6.8 million in two separate anti-drug operat...

item.title: The Impact of Climate Change on Respiratory Health in South Africa
item.description: In South Africa, the burden of respiratory illnesses is growing, compounded by the effects of climate change. While infectious diseases such as tuberculosis and HIV-related lung infections remain prev...

item.title: AEPB Calls For Better Performance From Abuja Waste Contractors
item.description: The Abuja Environmental Protection Board (AEPB) has raised concern over the low performance of the waste contractors hired by the Federal Capital Territory Administration. During a meeting with contra...

item.title: The Evolution of Gambling in Africa: From Traditional Games to the Online Era
item.description: Gambling has deep roots in African culture, tracing back thousands of years to traditional games like Mancala and Abbia. These early forms of play, often linked to social gatherings and wagers, illust...

item.title: Government Pushes for Copyright Law Changes as Artists Push Back Against AI Use of Their Work
item.description: The Australian federal government is calling for changes to copyright laws to accommodate the growing use of artificial intelligence (AI). Assistant Minister for Technology Andrew Charlton, spearheadi...

item.title: Meningitis B outbreak in Kent: vaccinations extended to Year 11 pupils at affected schools
item.description: UK health authorities have announced that Meningitis B vaccinations will be extended to Year 11 pupils at four schools in Kent as a precautionary measure to provide longer-term protection following a ...

item.title: Court Shifts El-Rufai Bail Hearing To March 31 After Arguments From Both Sides
item.description: A Federal High Court wey dey Kaduna don move the bail hearing of former Kaduna State governor, Nasir El-Rufai, go March 31, 2026. This one happen after both di prosecution and defence lawyers show the...

item.title: S&P Global PMI Shows Sharpest Rise in UK Manufacturing Input Costs Since 1992 Amid Iran War Disruptions
item.description: A flash S&P Global Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) survey released on March 24, 2026, reveals that British businesses experienced the slowest pace of activity growth in six months during March due to...

item.title: Sadapay app down due to disruption in AWS services during Middle East conflict
item.description: Sadapay, a Pakistani fintech company, announced on March 24, 2026, that its app was unavailable for users due to disruptions at Amazon Web Services (AWS) in Bahrain. This disruption occurred amidst th...

item.title: Pakistan Offers to Facilitate US-Iran Talks Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has stated that Pakistan is ready and honoured to host meaningful and conclusive talks between the United States and Iran. The announcement was made via a post on X, emph...

item.title: Jehovah's Witnesses Adjust Blood Transfusion Policy to Allow Use of Own Stored Blood
item.description: For many years, Jehovah’s Witnesses no allow blood transfusion because their faith teach say blood na sacred and Bible forbid am. Dem believe say accepting blood from another person na sin, and even s...

item.title: AIADMK Unveils Election Manifesto with 297 Promises for Tamil Nadu Assembly Polls
item.description: In preparation for the upcoming Tamil Nadu Assembly elections scheduled for April 23, 2026, Edappadi K Palaniswami, the general secretary of AIADMK and leader of the opposition, released the party’s m...

item.title: SC Justice Singh Encourages Women in Judiciary to Maximize Their Opportunities
item.description: Supreme Court Associate Justice Maria Filomena Singh has advised women in the judiciary to embrace their autonomy and make the most of the opportunities available to them. Speaking at the first Distin...

item.title: Thunder Bay First Nations medical transport program ends due to insufficient federal funding
item.description: The Sioux Lookout First Nations Health Authority (SLFNHA) will suspend its medical transportation program in Thunder Bay, Ontario, starting April 1, 2026, citing a lack of funding from Indigenous Serv...

item.title: Heavy Rains and Floods Leave 81 Dead and Thousands Displaced Across Kenya
item.description: At least 81 people have lost their lives across Kenya this March following weeks of heavy rains and flash floods that have wreaked havoc in many parts of the country. The Kenya National Police confirm...

item.title: Reflecting on César Chávez's Legacy Amid New Allegations
item.description: In the wake of new sexual assault allegations against César Chávez, many are reevaluating his legacy and removing his name and image from public spaces. Despite this, columnist Gustavo Arellano decide...

item.title: EU and Australia conclude free trade agreement with defence and minerals cooperation after eight years
item.description: The European Union and Australia have finally signed a comprehensive free trade deal on Tuesday after eight long years of tough negotiations. EU Commission President Ursula von der Leyen visited Austr...

item.title: Understanding the Swahili Term 'Kiti Mwendo' and Its Usage
item.description: This article explains the meaning of the Swahili phrase 'kiti mwendo', which literally translates to 'moving chair' and refers to a wheelchair in English. A wheelchair is a chair with wheels used by p...
